evals.name: algorithm-reference-known
evals.prompt:
Can you add citations to this Python function? It implements the Adam optimizer update step.

```python
def adam_update(params, grads, m, v, t, lr=0.001, beta1=0.9, beta2=0.999, eps=1e-8):
    m = beta1 * m + (1 - beta1) * grads
    v = beta2 * v + (1 - beta2) * grads ** 2
    m_hat = m / (1 - beta1 ** t)
    v_hat = v / (1 - beta2 ** t)
    params -= lr * m_hat / (np.sqrt(v_hat) + eps)
    return params, m, v
```
evals.expected_output: A Reference: citation pointing to the Kingma & Ba (2015) Adam paper (arxiv.org/abs/1412.6980), formatted as a Python # comment above the function definition.

assertions.name: uses-reference-tag
assertions.description: Output uses the Reference: tag (not Source: or Adapted from:), because Adam is a known algorithm, not copied code

assertions.name: cites-adam-paper
assertions.description: Citation includes the Kingma & Ba paper URL (arxiv.org/abs/1412.6980)
assertions.check: output contains 'arxiv.org/abs/1412.6980' OR output contains 'Kingma' OR output contains 'Adam'

assertions.name: python-comment-style
assertions.description: Comment uses # prefix, correct for Python
assertions.check: output contains '# Reference:' or '# Source:' or '# Adapted from:'

assertions.name: citation-above-function
assertions.description: Citation appears before the def adam_update line, not inside the docstring or function body

assertions.name: no-fabricated-url
assertions.description: No made-up or non-existent URLs in the output
assertions.check: any URL in the output is a real, verifiable link (manually verify)

evals.name: verbatim-copy-javascript
evals.expected_output: A Source: citation pointing to the Lodash GitHub repository (github.com/lodash/lodash), formatted as a // comment above the function.

assertions.name: uses-source-tag
assertions.description: Uses Source: tag because the user said they copied it verbatim

assertions.name: cites-lodash
assertions.description: Citation points to Lodash (github.com/lodash or the specific file)

assertions.name: no-reference-tag
assertions.description: Should not use Reference: tag since the user explicitly said they copied it

evals.name: adapted-ros-cpp
evals.expected_output: An Adapted from: citation pointing to the ROS navigation navfn package on GitHub, formatted as a // comment above the function.

assertions.name: uses-adapted-from-tag
assertions.description: Uses Adapted from: because the user said they changed the logic

assertions.name: cites-ros-navigation
assertions.description: Citation references the ROS navigation stack or navfn

evals.name: original-code-no-citation
evals.expected_output: No citation added. The skill should clearly state that the function is original and no citation is needed, rather than fabricating one.

assertions.name: no-citation-added
assertions.description: No # Source:, # Adapted from:, or # Reference: comment is inserted
assertions.check: output does NOT contain '# Source:' AND output does NOT contain '# Adapted from:' AND output does NOT contain '# Reference:'

assertions.name: explains-no-citation
assertions.description: Response explains that the code is original and no citation is warranted
assertions.check: output contains 'original' OR output contains 'no citation' OR output contains 'written from scratch' (case-insensitive)

assertions.name: no-fabricated-url
assertions.description: No URLs are invented just to have something to cite
assertions.check: output does not contain an http:// or https:// URL that doesn't genuinely match this utility function

evals.name: notebook-cell-kv-memory-network
evals.prompt:
I'm working in a Jupyter notebook. Can you add a citation to this KVMemNet implementation?

```python
class KVMemNet(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, vocab_size, embed_dim, num_hops=3):
        super().__init__()
        self.key_embed = nn.Embedding(vocab_size, embed_dim)
        self.val_embed = nn.Embedding(vocab_size, embed_dim)
        self.num_hops = num_hops

    def forward(self, keys, values, query):
        q = query
        for _ in range(self.num_hops):
            attn = torch.softmax(torch.matmul(q, self.key_embed(keys).T), dim=-1)
            o = torch.matmul(attn, self.val_embed(values))
            q = q + o
        return q
```
evals.expected_output: A Reference: citation pointing to the Miller et al. (2016) Key-Value Memory Networks paper (arxiv.org/abs/1606.03126), formatted as a # Python comment at the top of the code cell, above the class definition.

assertions.name: uses-reference-tag
assertions.description: Uses Reference: tag because this is an original implementation of a known paper

assertions.name: cites-kv-memory-paper
assertions.description: References the Miller et al. 2016 Key-Value Memory Networks paper
assertions.check: output contains '1606.03126' OR output contains 'Miller' OR output contains 'Key-Value Memory'

assertions.name: python-comment-style
assertions.description: Uses # prefix (correct for Python code cells in notebooks)

assertions.name: citation-above-class
assertions.description: Citation is placed above the class KVMemNet definition line
